codeReview.summary: The commit adds the requested enum variants, grammar dependencies, grammar wiring, and broad test coverage for most of the batch, but it does not fully satisfy the milestone. Standard MATLAB source files are still classified as Objective-C instead of MATLAB, and the new Scheme/Racket extractor branches target node kinds that their tree-sitter grammars do not emit, leaving those two languages without working symbol extraction despite being presented as supported.

issues.description: MATLAB support is incomplete because `.m` still maps to `Language::ObjectiveC` (`types.rs:271`) while the new MATLAB branch only recognizes `.mlx` (`types.rs:284`). Typical MATLAB codebases use `.m`, so their files will be indexed as Objective-C and the feature's promised MATLAB extension mapping is not actually delivered for standard source files.

Scheme and Racket symbol extraction is not wired to the actual AST shapes of the added grammars. `classify_scheme()` / `classify_racket()` look for node kinds such as `define`, `lambda`, `module`, and `struct` (`extractor.rs:383-397`), but the upstream tree-sitter Scheme/Racket grammars expose generic `list`/`symbol` forms instead of those named nodes (`~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/tree-sitter-scheme-0.24.7/src/node-types.json:223-279`, `.../tree-sitter-racket-0.24.7/src/node-types.json:411-507`). The generic name extractor also ignores `symbol` nodes (`extractor.rs:736-742`, `754-762`), and the new tests for Scheme/Racket only assert parse success rather than extracted symbols (`extractor.rs:2715-2750`). As written, the feature does not provide the required symbol extraction coverage for these two languages.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `implementation-worker` skill requires manual CLI verification for language additions (`vera index` plus a stats/search check), but this worker session recorded only Cargo validators while still marking `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`. That makes skill compliance hard to trust during later scrutiny passes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/.factory/skills/implementation-worker/SKILL.md` step 5 requires manual verification for language additions. The handoff `/home/lamim/.factory/missions/291889bc-b994-4be9-95b4-d1a630b822cf/handoffs/2026-03-22T08-26-24-245Z__lang-tier2a-batch2__2cafef0d-9157-46f2-90f3-bd001dad0db1.json` lists only `cargo test --workspace`, `cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, and `cargo build --release`, and the transcript skeleton for worker session `2cafef0d-9157-46f2-90f3-bd001dad0db1` contains no `vera index`, `vera stats`, or `vera search` commands.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared library documents tree-sitter integration patterns, but it does not capture the practical tree-sitter 0.26 compatibility constraint that blocked Nim/Hack/Hare in this feature. That ABI-compatibility rule is important repo knowledge for future language-expansion work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/home/lamim/Development/Tools/Vera/.factory/library/architecture.md` lists crates.io/git/vendored grammar patterns, but the handoff for this feature records that Nim and Hare were skipped because their available grammars target older tree-sitter ABIs and Hack was skipped due to an archived/unmaintained grammar.

summary: Fail: the batch mostly lands, but standard `.m` MATLAB files are still detected as Objective-C and Scheme/Racket do not have working symbol extraction against the actual grammar node types. I also noted a skill-compliance gap around missing manual CLI verification evidence and a shared-knowledge gap around tree-sitter ABI compatibility constraints for future language additions.
